MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES (TRANSACTION ID # 62793992) FILED BY DEFENDANT PATEL, KELLY

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AT DEC-21-2018 HEARING, DEFENDANT STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING HEARD BY THE PRO TEM JUDGE. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: ALTHOUGH NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES, TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED AND FOR SANCTIONS, THE COURT IS COMPELLED TO DENY THE MOTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO IT BEING REFILED AND RE-NOTICED. THE INITIAL NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES, TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED, REQUESTS FOR SANCTION WHICH WAS FILED ON NOV.20, 2018 STATED THAT IT WOULD "MOVE THE COURT FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT TO RESPOND" OBVIOUSLY, FROM THE PAPERS THIS WAS AN INCORRECT STATEMENT, BUT WHERE THE PLAINTIFF IS SELF-REPRESENTED, THE COURT BELIEVES IT IS INCUMBENT UPON THE COURT TO REQUIRE DEFENDANT TO REFILE AND RE-NOTICE WITH THE CORRECT STATEMENT OF THE RELIEF SOUGHT. COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT A COPY TO PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER IT TO THE COURT FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR AUG-26-2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JAN-16-2019 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY 302, ARGUED. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT KELLY PATEL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES, TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: JASON YURASEK, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)
MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR AUG-26-2019 VACATED. DISMISSAL FILED 03/04/19. (206)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gary Dean Converse (415-756-2569) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Howard Olsen, esq. (415-931-4207) of Law Offices of Michael Heath for Defendant Kelly Patel.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT KELLY PATEL's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted Request For Sanctions is Granted.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Jason Yurasek, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-02-2019 Set For Trial On Aug-26-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted, Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ALEXANDER J. PEREZ, ESQ. of Law Offices of Alexander J. Perez (Tel: 415-682-2540) specially appearing for defendant kelly patel;
No other appearances made.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Although no opposition was filed to defendant's motion to compel discovery responses, to deem matters admitted and for sanctions, the court is compelled to deny the motion without prejudice to it being refiled and re-noticed. The initial notice of motion to compel discovery responses, to deem matters admitted, requests for sanction which was filed on nov.20, 2018 stated that it would "move the court for an order compelling defendant to respond" obviously, from the papers this was an incorrect statement, but where the plaintiff is self-represented, the Court believes it is incumbent upon the court to require defendant to refile and re-notice with the correct statement of the relief sought.

Counsel is to prepare an appropriate order, submit a copy to plaintiff for approval as to form and deliver it to the court for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/JPT)
